title: What does the music player scene in episode 9 mean
display_title: What does the music player scene in episode 9 mean
wikitext:
[]: What does the music player scene in episode 9 mean?             Quote Post by Platformnik » Tue Apr 20, 2010 7:23 pm I have a basic understanding of what the music player means in general (Shinji shutting out reality/possibly something about accepting Instrumentality) but in episode 9 the scene after Shinji and Asuka go to bed I never really fully understood. It's the only time (if I recall correctly) Shinji's music player is on something besides 25 and 26 and is rewinding back far. Does Asuka really start sleeping next to Shinji after going to the bathroom? Or is it Shinji that goes to Asuka's room, since you see later that Asuka's on her bed and Shinji's on the floor?

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Tue Apr 20, 2010 7:28 pm http://wiki.evageeks.org/FGC:Supplemental_(The_Significance_of_Shinji%27s_SDAT) Pretty much explained right there.

[]:             Quote Post by Platformnik » Tue Apr 20, 2010 9:08 pm ran1 wrote: http://wiki.evageeks.org/FGC:Supplemental_(The_Significance_of_Shinji%27s_SDAT) Pretty much explained right there.

[]: Re: What does the music player scene in episode 9 mean?             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Apr 20, 2010 9:20 pm Platformnik wrote: Does Asuka really start sleeping next to Shinji after going to the bathroom? Or is it Shinji that goes to Asuka's room, since you see later that Asuka's on her bed and Shinji's on the floor?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Apr 20, 2010 11:07 pm Yeah, the SDAT does seem to echo Shinji's surprise in that scene. The rapidity of it rewinding is probably meant to symbolize Shinji's quickening heartbeat. That's how I always took it.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Apr 21, 2010 1:36 am For the non-SDAT related parts of the original set of questions, this thread http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=684 is a good starting point for "what happened where" that evening.

[]: Re: What does the music player scene in episode 9 mean?             Quote Post by Platformnik » Wed Apr 21, 2010 2:34 pm Sachi wrote: Just to clear this up: Shinji abandoned his own bed and let Asuka unconsciously conquer it. Being that it's Shinji, of course he's not going to wake her up and say something like "Uh, hello, excuse me; this is my bed, bitch. Go back to your precious Wall of Jericho." As for the SDAT, the sudden rewinding is of a result of Shinji's sudden surprise. I would guess that the symbolism may relate to the expression, "my heart jumped backwards," meaning that during those few moments Shinji was in complete shock (durr), and the rewinding track represents his heart, which has stopped beating forward. This whole scene holds a high tension, which would leave Shinji in a huge amount of shock and/or excitement as he's trying to kiss Asuka. However, when the track stops rewinding, so does he, and he moves over to sleep on the floor. Just my haphazardly organized two cents.
